Vanadium, Chromium, Iron and Nickel Up to 14 MeV

Metallic Foils of Natural Titanium Containing 73.9%
Ti-48, Thickness 2 to 5 Mg/Cm**2.
From the Given Decay Probability of B+ Emission It is
Assumed That the Coincidence of the Annihilation
Radiation was Measured - Comment by the Compiler of
Kachapag.
Used For the Determination of the Disintegration Rate
After Decay of the Short-Lived Interferences.
Used For Relative Measurements
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
By Kachapag.
Metallic Foils of Natural Titanium Containing 7.28%
Ti-47, Thickness 2 to 5 Mg/Cm**2.
.For Measurement of the Ratios V-47/V-48 from
Annihilation Radiation. V-47 Cross Sections were
Given by Applying these Ratios to the Absolute Cross
Sections of V-48.
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
By Kachapag.
Slurries of Vanadium Pentoxide in Clear Lacquer
Were Spattered onto Thin Al-Foils of Known Thickness.
Isotopic Abundance 99.8% V-51.
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
By Kachapag
Sum Cross Sections For Isomer and Ground State
Production.
Metallic Foils of Natural Chromium Containing 83.8%
Cr-52, Prepared by Electrodeposition.
From the Given Decay Probability of B+ Emission It is
Assumed That the Coincidence of the Annihilation
Radiation was Measured - Comment by the Compiler of
Kachapag.
Used For the Determination of the Disintegration Rate
After Decay of the Short-Lived Interferences.
Used For Relative Measurements
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
By Kachapag.
Metallic Foils of Natural Iron Containing 91.7% Fe-56,
Thickness 2 to 5 Mg/Cm**2.
A 4 Pi Methane Proportional Counter was Used For the
Determination of the Disintegration Rate, While
Relative Measurements were Obtained with a Na-I
Scintillation Counter.
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
The Symmetric Error Given in Ornl-Cpx-1 was Changed
Into the Unsymmetric Error According to the Publication
By the Compiler of Kachapag.
The 130 KeV Gamma Ray Gives the Sum of the Unresolved
Rays of 122 and 136 KeV.
Metallic Foils of Natural Iron Containing 2.19% Fe-57,
Thickness 2 to 5 Mg/Cm**2.
.Well Crystal
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
The Symmetric Error Given in Ornl-Cpx-1 was Changed
Into the Unsymmetric Error According to the Publication
By the Compiler.
Metallic Foils of Natural Nickel Containing 1.19%
Ni-61, Thickness 2 to 5 Mg/Cm**2.
By the Gm-Counter the Cu-61/Cu-64 Ratios were Measured.
The Cu-61 Cross Sections were Obtained from these
Ratios and the Disintegration Rate of Cu-64.
The Energy Loss in Aluminium was Calculated from the
Range-Energy Curve of W.A.Aron Et Al., Aecu-663(1949).
The Energy Loss in Other Foils was Calculated from
The Stopping Power Formula of H.A.Bethe, Rev.Mod.Phys.
By Kachapag.
Shielded.
The Abundance of 0.57 Gives the Probability of B+ and
B- Emission.
